Home.title: OpenClaw Installation Guide
Home.description: OpenClaw is a powerful personal AI assistant framework. This tutorial guides you through installation on personal computers (local) and cloud servers (remote).

section1.title: I. Personal Computer Installation (Windows / macOS / Linux)
section1.intro: The easiest way to install OpenClaw on a personal computer is using the official one-click installation script.

requirements.title: 1. System Requirements
requirements.nodejs: Node.js: Version 22 or higher required (script will auto-detect and attempt installation).

windows.title: Windows (PowerShell)
windows.desc: Open PowerShell as Administrator and run:

verify.title: 3. Verify Installation
verify.desc: After installation, verify with these commands:

section2.title: II. Cloud Server Installation (Linux / Docker)
section2.intro: On cloud servers (VPS), we recommend Docker for containerized deployment to keep the environment clean and manageable.

requirements.title: 1. Prerequisites
requirements.os: A cloud server running a mainstream Linux distro (e.g., Ubuntu 22.04 LTS).

deploy.title: 2. Quick Deploy with Docker
deploy.intro: Recommended: Use the official docker-setup.sh script:
deploy.step2_desc: This script automatically builds images, runs the init wizard, and starts services.
deploy.step3_desc: For finer control, use these commands:

access.title: 3. Access & Config
access.dashboard: Dashboard: Default at http://ServerIP:18789/.
access.security: Security: Set a strong password or use SSH tunneling immediately after deployment.

issue1.title: 1. openclaw command not found
issue1.desc: Check environment variables if command is missing.
issue1.unix: macOS/Linux: Add $(npm prefix -g)/bin to your PATH (usually in ~/.zshrc or ~/.bashrc).
issue1.windows: Windows: Add npm prefix -g output path to System Path.

issue2.title: 2. Permission Issues
issue2.desc: On Linux/macOS, try prepending sudo or checking folder ownership.

section4.title: IV. Update & Uninstall
section4.update: Update: Run openclaw update or re-run install script.
section4.uninstall: Uninstall: Run openclaw uninstall.

timestamp.name: Timestamp Converter
timestamp.desc: Convert Unix timestamps to human-readable dates and vice versa.
